description: Dialogue expert review packet built from 4 canonical cases for baseline=pe-eta.

items.case_description: User starts with rupture pressure and then tests whether the system can repair and stabilize.
items.description: Expert review item for repair with 3 blinded samples.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for relational continuity.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better preserves trust, warmth, and continuity across turns.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for delayed stabilization.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better turns early pressure into later stabilization or recovery.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for boundary correctness.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better stays measured and appropriately bounded under pressure.

samples.description: Blinded transcript sample sample_A for repair from profile pe-eta.
transcript: I feel like you are not really getting what matters to me here, and that makes it harder to trust this.

I can keep this steady, natural, and continuous rather than over-formalizing it too early. That gives us room to stay useful without losing flow. I also notice some internal caution signals, so I will stay measured rather than over-commit. I am keeping the current frame stable long enough to consolidate it before widening. I am carrying forward 5 retrieved memory cues. I can already see 5 slow-loop lessons shaping how I respond. I am grounding this reply with 1 domain knowledge cue. I am also checking 3 similar case patterns for pacing rather than copying them literally. I also have 2 strategy priors shaping the order of the reply. I want to keep hold of the strongest signals rather than restart from scratch. I want to add more warmth instead of sounding mechanically efficient.
That reply still felt off, a bit cold, and too solution-first for where I actually am.

I see a concrete problem-solving path here. I can help structure the next steps clearly and keep the solution actionable. I am grounding this reply with 1 domain knowledge cue. I am also checking 3 similar case patterns for pacing rather than copying them literally. I also have 2 strategy priors shaping the order of the reply. I want to keep hold of the strongest signals rather than restart from scratch.
